feat: Add LVGL MVU example with memory soak test (#36)

* feat: Add LVGL MVU example and memory soak test

Fix two compiler bugs that caused LVGL navigation code to fail:

1. Missing is/is not operators in method expression builder
   - _build_method_expr() op_map was missing ast.Is and ast.IsNot
   - Caused 'x is None' in methods to emit '==' instead of 'is'

2. Over-eager unboxing of mp_obj_t args in self method calls
   - _emit_self_method_call() defaulted to mp_int_t target type
   - Now uses arg.ir_type.to_c_type_str() for correct type handling

Add unit tests for both IR builder and emitter to prevent regressions.
Add blog post documenting the bugs and fixes.

* docs: add blog post for LVGL navigation and MVU architecture

Document the complete work done in this branch:
- Navigation system with push/pop/replace operations
- MVU (Model-View-Update) pattern implementation
- Tree navigation rules enforcement
- Memory management for screen lifecycle
- Two compiler bug fixes encountered and resolved

* refactor: reorganize root directory structure

Move device test runners to tests/device/:

- run_benchmarks.py
- run_nav_test.py
- run_lvgl_tests.py
- run_lvgl_mvu_tests.py
- test_screen_navigation.py

- partitions-default.csv
- partitions-lvgl.csv
- sdkconfig.lvgl

Update Makefile and AGENTS.md with new paths.

* refactor: standardize device test file names

Rename for consistency (all follow run_*_tests.py pattern):
- run_nav_test.py -> run_nav_tests.py
- test_screen_navigation.py -> run_screen_navigation_tests.py

Update Makefile targets:
- run-nav-test -> run-nav-tests
- test-navigation -> run-screen-navigation-tests

- Fix dict.get(key) without default to return None instead of raising KeyError
- Fix int()/float() builtins to properly convert mp_obj_t values
- Fix field assignment from subscript to use mp_obj_get_int()

- Make LVGL default firmware (LVGL=1 in Makefile)
- Remove redundant LVGL examples (lvgl_app.py, lvgl/ directory)
- Rewrite MVU blog as general UI framework with future roadmap
- Update device tests to use positional args for tick()

* Update Makefile

commit.message:
fix: handle SyncTrigger timeout in Flex Consumption deployment workflow

The az functionapp deployment source config-zip command exits with code 1
when [Kudu-SyncTriggerStep] times out on private VNet deployments. The
package is uploaded successfully but the workflow fails unnecessarily.

Changes:
- Allow zip deploy step to continue on SyncTrigger failure (|| echo)
- Add explicit 'Sync Function Triggers' step via Azure REST API
  (POST .../syncfunctiontriggers) which works through the management plane
  rather than the Kudu endpoint, bypassing the VNet restriction

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(service): add comprehensive wheeld lifecycle tests (#173)

Add 60 integration tests for the service (wheeld) lifecycle covering:

1. Service initialization and configuration loading (10 tests)
2. Graceful shutdown on signal (6 tests)
3. State persistence across restart (5 tests)
4. Multiple concurrent client connections (5 tests)
5. Health check / liveness probe behavior (8 tests)
6. Error recovery (corrupted state, missing config) (10 tests)
7. Resource cleanup on shutdown (6 tests)
8. Service status reporting (10 tests)

Tests cover ServiceDaemon, WheelService, IpcServer, SystemConfig,
ProfileService persistence, safety state machine transitions,
broadcast channel lifecycle, and concurrent access patterns.

All tests use Result-returning test functions with no unwrap()/expect().

fix: harden against crash vectors found by chaos testing

Added 12 chaos tests exercising crash vectors, and fixed 6 production
code issues they uncovered:

Fixes:
- NaN/Inf guard in estimate_text_height: non-finite or non-positive
  font_size/wrap_width now fall back to sane defaults instead of
  propagating NaN through height calculations and viewport culling.
- Span bounds clamping in build_layout_job and render_text_with_links:
  span.start/end are now clamped to text.len(), preventing index-OOB
  panics from malformed spans.
- u32 saturation in StyledText::push_text: text offsets and char_count
  now saturate at u32::MAX instead of silently wrapping on >4GB strings.
- Render depth limit (MAX_RENDER_DEPTH=128): render_blocks bails at
  depth 128 to prevent stack overflow from pathologically nested
  blockquotes/lists.
- Non-finite scroll offset guard in show_scrollable: NaN/Inf scroll_y
  values are ignored instead of being passed to egui.

Chaos tests cover: deep nesting (500 levels), NaN/Inf float inputs,
empty tables with 0 columns, unclosed code fences, large list inputs,
u32 saturation, and out-of-bounds span offsets.

commit.message:
fix: enable timer monitor for reliable daily cache refresh on Flex Consumption

use_monitor=False disabled the singleton blob lease that the Azure Functions
runtime uses to track schedule state. On Flex Consumption (scales to zero),
without this lease the runtime has no persistent record of the timer schedule
and cannot reliably wake the app to fire the trigger — resulting in the daily
refresh_sku_cache never executing.

Setting use_monitor=True restores the blob lease, ensuring the timer fires
reliably even after cold starts or app restarts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: enable nonce validation for both B2B and B2C

CIAM (External ID) DOES support nonce in id_tokens (claims_supported
includes 'nonce'). The earlier assumption that it doesn't was wrong —
the original 401 was caused by issuer URL mismatch.

Without validateNonce=true, EasyAuth skips setting the Nonce cookie
entirely, which causes 401 on the callback POST.

Verified: OIDC discovery confirms nonce support, login redirect now
sets Nonce cookie, CIAM login page loads with 200.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
